question: When is the best time to apply body lotion?
options: Only before going to bed
Right after showering while skin is still slightly damp to lock in moisture
Only when skin feels very dry and itchy
First thing in the morning before showering

question: What is the main difference between body lotion and body cream?
options: They are exactly the same product in different packaging
Body lotion is thinner and absorbs faster while body cream is thicker and more nourishing for very dry skin
Body cream is only for the face and lotion is only for the body
Body lotion contains SPF and body cream does not

question: What is a body butter?
options: A cooking butter used in DIY skincare
A very rich thick moisturiser often made with shea cocoa or mango butter that deeply nourishes very dry skin
A lightweight lotion for oily body skin
A body wash that also moisturises

question: Why should you moisturise right after showering?
options: Because shower water adds extra nutrients to skin
Because skin loses moisture quickly after washing and applying lotion on slightly damp skin helps seal hydration in
Because soap makes skin permanently dry and lotion fixes it
Because hot water opens pores that need to be sealed immediately

question: Can you use face moisturiser on your body?
options: Yes they are the same product and work identically
Technically yes but face moisturisers are expensive for the larger body surface area and body lotions are formulated for thicker body skin
Face moisturisers are always too strong for body skin
Face moisturisers never absorb into body skin

question: Can you use body lotion on your face?
options: Yes body and face lotions are always interchangeable
Generally no as body lotions can be too heavy and contain ingredients that clog facial pores
Body lotion is always better than face moisturiser
Only use body lotion on the face if you run out of face cream

question: What is the best ingredient to look for in a hand cream for very dry and cracked hands?
options: Salicylic acid
Alcohol
Shea butter glycerin or urea which deeply moisturise and repair dry cracked skin
Fragrance and essential oils

question: How often should you apply hand cream throughout the day?
options: Only once before bed
Every time you wash your hands as washing removes natural oils and hand cream restores moisture
Only when hands look visibly cracked
Once a week is plenty for most people

question: What ingredient in body wash helps maintain skin moisture while cleansing?
options: Alcohol
Fragrance
Sulfates
Glycerin or moisturising oils that prevent the wash from being too stripping

question: What body wash ingredient should people with sensitive skin try to avoid?
options: Glycerin
Aloe vera
Sodium lauryl sulfate and synthetic fragrances which can irritate sensitive skin
Shea butter

question: What is urea used for in body lotions and creams?
options: It provides a pleasant fragrance to the product
It is a powerful humectant that attracts water to skin and also gently softens and exfoliates very dry rough skin
It provides SPF protection to body skin
It kills bacteria on the skin surface

question: What is the best way to treat very dry cracked heels?
options: Only apply regular body lotion once a week
Apply a thick foot cream with urea or salicylic acid at night and wear socks to lock in moisture while sleeping
Soak heels in hot water every day for hours
Only see a professional and never treat at home

question: What is a body scrub used for?
options: Moisturising and nourishing body skin
Physically exfoliating dead skin cells from the body to reveal smoother softer skin underneath
Cleansing the body instead of body wash
Providing SPF protection to body skin

question: How often should you exfoliate your body with a scrub?
options: Every single day in the shower
Once or twice a week is enough for most people to keep skin smooth without over-exfoliating
Only once a month
Every morning and every night

question: What household ingredient makes a great DIY body scrub?
options: Baking soda mixed with bleach
Sugar or coffee grounds mixed with a little coconut oil or olive oil
Flour and water paste
Lemon juice and vinegar

question: Should you apply sunscreen to body skin as well as your face?
options: No sunscreen is only needed on the face
Yes any exposed body skin like arms legs and chest needs sunscreen too
Only when wearing a swimsuit at the beach
Only in summer months on very hot days

question: What is body oil and how is it different from body lotion?
options: Body oil is thicker than body lotion
Body oil is a lightweight oil that nourishes and adds a glow but may need a moisturiser as well for very dry skin while lotion contains both water and oil
Body oil contains SPF and body lotion does not
Body oil is only for after-sun use

question: What is the best order to use body oil and body lotion?
options: Body oil first then body lotion on top
Body lotion first then body oil on top to seal in moisture
They should never be used together
Mix them together before applying

question: What part of the body is most often forgotten when moisturising?
options: Arms and legs
The elbows knees heels and back of the neck which are prone to dryness
The stomach
The chest and décolletage

question: Why do elbows and knees get especially dry and rough?
options: Because they produce the most oil
Because the skin in these areas is thicker and there are no oil glands making them prone to dryness and rough patches
Because elbows and knees are always exposed to the sun
Because people apply too much lotion to these areas

question: What is a body mist and how is it different from body lotion?
options: A body mist is a thick cream in spray form
A body mist is a light fragrant spray that adds a subtle scent and a very light layer of moisture unlike the deeper hydration of lotion
A body mist replaces deodorant
A body mist contains SPF for sun protection

question: What should you look for in a body wash if you have eczema-prone skin?
options: The most fragranced option for a spa-like experience
A fragrance-free soap-free gentle formula designed for sensitive or eczema-prone skin
A deeply exfoliating body wash to remove dry patches
A body wash with the highest lather possible

question: What is the purpose of a tanning lotion or gradual self-tanner?
options: To protect skin from UV rays
To gradually build a natural-looking tan on skin without sun exposure
To moisturise skin with an SPF
To treat stretch marks and uneven body skin tone

question: What does dry brushing do for body skin?
options: Deeply moisturises body skin
Physically exfoliates dead skin cells stimulates circulation and is said to reduce the appearance of cellulite temporarily
Removes body hair effectively
Provides UV protection to body skin

question: What direction should you dry brush for best results?
options: In random circular motions anywhere
Toward the heart in long sweeping strokes to support lymphatic drainage and circulation
Away from the heart starting at the face
Only on the legs and never on the upper body

question: What is a stretch mark and can creams completely remove them?
options: Stretch marks are a type of scar from rapid skin stretching and creams can completely remove them
Stretch marks are scars from rapid growth and no cream can completely remove them though they may improve appearance over time
Stretch marks are only caused by pregnancy
Stretch marks always disappear within one year without treatment

question: What ingredient in stretch mark creams may help improve their appearance over time?
options: Salicylic acid
Retinol centella asiatica and hyaluronic acid which support collagen and hydration
Benzoyl peroxide
Glycolic acid used daily at full strength

question: What is bacne and how can body wash help treat it?
options: Bacne is a back rash caused by allergies
Bacne is acne on the back and a body wash containing salicylic acid can help treat it by exfoliating pores and reducing bacteria
Bacne is body eczema not acne
Bacne only affects teenagers and goes away without treatment

question: What should you do before applying a self-tanning product for an even result?
options: Apply it straight onto dry unprepped skin
Exfoliate skin and moisturise dry areas like elbows and knees to create a smooth even base for the self-tanner
Apply sunscreen underneath the self-tanner
Wet skin thoroughly right before applying

question: Why do hands age more visibly than other body parts?
options: Because hands produce more oil than other areas
Because hands are constantly exposed to sun water soap and environmental damage while also losing volume with age
Because hand skin is always thicker and harder
Because people never moisturise their hands

question: Should you wear SPF on the back of your hands?
options: No hands do not need sun protection
Yes the backs of hands are frequently exposed to sun and are one of the first areas to show age spots and sun damage
Only in beach environments
Only people who drive need hand SPF

question: What is the best way to get rid of keratosis pilaris the small rough bumps often on arms and thighs?
options: Scrubbing very hard every day to remove the bumps
Using a gentle chemical exfoliant with AHA or BHA plus a rich moisturiser consistently to smooth bumps over time
Keratosis pilaris cannot be improved with skincare at all
Only professional laser treatments help keratosis pilaris

question: What is keratosis pilaris commonly nicknamed?
options: Goose skin
Chicken skin because the small bumps resemble the skin of a plucked chicken
Strawberry skin
Sandpaper skin

question: What does a rich foot cream with urea do for hard skin on feet?
options: Only moisturises without any other benefit
Urea softens and gently dissolves hard thickened skin making feet smoother and easier to maintain
Urea provides SPF protection to feet
Urea exfoliates feet the same way a physical scrub does

question: What is a good overnight hand treatment for very dry damaged hands?
options: Apply nothing and let hands rest
Apply a thick hand cream or petroleum jelly then wear cotton gloves overnight to lock in intense moisture while you sleep
Apply a thin layer of regular lotion right before sleeping
Soak hands in water overnight

question: What body area should women especially remember to apply SPF to daily?
options: The underarms
The chest and décolletage which is often exposed and highly prone to sun damage and premature aging
The back of the knees
The inner arms

question: What is an exfoliating body wash?
options: A body wash with moisturising beads
A body wash that contains chemical or physical exfoliants to cleanse and remove dead skin at the same time
A body wash that contains SPF
A body wash that also works as a shaving cream

question: What is the purpose of a shower oil?
options: To provide fragrance while showering
To cleanse skin while leaving a nourishing protective layer of oil that prevents moisture loss unlike regular soap
To exfoliate skin during showering
To replace body lotion completely

question: What skin condition benefits most from a body wash with colloidal oatmeal?
options: Oily body skin
Eczema and sensitive irritated skin as colloidal oatmeal has soothing anti-inflammatory properties
Acne-prone back skin
Keratosis pilaris on arms and thighs

question: What is a good tip for applying body lotion to your back which is hard to reach?
options: Skip your back as it does not need moisturising
Use a long-handled lotion applicator or ask someone to help or focus on reachable areas and use a spray lotion for hard-to-reach spots
Only apply lotion to the back in winter
Back skin never needs moisturiser

question: What does AHA in a body lotion do for rough bumpy body skin?
options: Adds fragrance to the body lotion formula
Chemically exfoliates dead skin cells to smooth rough patches and improve texture over time
Provides SPF protection to body skin
Kills bacteria on body skin to prevent odour

question: How should you treat skin after hair removal like waxing or shaving on the body?

Apply a gentle fragrance-free soothing lotion to calm irritation and keep skin moisturised while avoiding active ingredients immediately after
Exfoliate immediately after waxing to prevent ingrown hairs
Apply body oil with alcohol to sanitise the skin

question: What helps prevent ingrown hairs after shaving?
options: Shaving against the grain for a closer shave
Regular gentle exfoliation to prevent dead skin cells from trapping hairs and moisturising consistently after shaving
Applying lemon juice to skin after shaving
Never moisturising after shaving

question: What ingredient in body products is best for very itchy dry body skin?
options: Fragrance and essential oils
Colloidal oatmeal aloe vera or ceramides which soothe calm and repair itchy irritated skin
Salicylic acid
Retinol

question: What is a body peel and how is it different from a body scrub?
options: A body peel uses physical particles and a scrub uses chemicals
A body peel uses chemical exfoliants like AHAs to dissolve dead skin while a body scrub uses physical particles to manually buff skin
They are exactly the same product with different names
A body peel is only used by professionals and never at home

question: When should you avoid using a body scrub or exfoliant?
options: In winter when skin needs more exfoliation
On sunburned irritated broken or freshly shaved skin as exfoliating compromised skin causes more damage and pain
On oily body skin
After applying body lotion

question: What is the benefit of adding a few drops of body oil to your bath water?
options: It cleans your skin more effectively
It leaves a light moisturising film on skin as you bathe helping to prevent dryness after getting out
It replaces the need for body lotion completely
Bath oils only work for people with dry skin types

question: What is the difference between a deodorant and an antiperspirant for underarm care?
options: They are exactly the same product
Deodorant masks odour while antiperspirant reduces sweating by blocking sweat glands temporarily
Antiperspirant is always natural and deodorant is always synthetic
Deodorant is only for women and antiperspirant is only for men

question: What should you look for in a deodorant if you have sensitive underarm skin?
options: The strongest formula with the most aluminium
An alcohol-free and fragrance-free formula designed for sensitive skin to prevent irritation and redness
Any strongly fragranced option for freshness
A product with the highest amount of baking soda available

question: What is the best overall habit for keeping body skin consistently soft and smooth year round?
options: Only moisturising when skin feels very dry
Consistently moisturising after every shower exfoliating regularly wearing SPF on exposed areas and drinking plenty of water daily
Only moisturising in winter months
Applying the most expensive body cream once a week
